stem: A coffee shop sells two types of coffee beans. The manager mixes premium beans costing $18 per pound with regular beans costing $12 per pound to create a 20-pound blend that costs $14.40 per pound. How many pounds of premium beans are used in the blend?
explanation: Let p = pounds of premium beans and r = pounds of regular beans. We can set up two equations: p + r = 20 (total weight) and 18p + 12r = 14.40(20) = 288 (total cost). From the first equation, r = 20 - p. Substituting into the second equation: 18p + 12(20 - p) = 288. Simplifying: 18p + 240 - 12p = 288, so 6p + 240 = 288. Subtracting 240 from both sides: 6p = 48. Dividing by 6: p = 8 pounds.

distractorRationale.A: Found the weight of regular beans instead of premium: r = 20 - 8 = 12.
distractorRationale.B: Assumed equal amounts of each bean: 20 / 2 = 10.
distractorRationale.C: Correct: Using p + r = 20 and 18p + 12r = 288, substitute r = 20 - p to get 18p + 12(20 - p) = 288, which simplifies to 6p = 48, so p = 8.
distractorRationale.D: Used the concentration ratio with half the total weight instead of the full weight: (18 - 14.40)/(18 - 12) = 3.60/6 = 0.6, then 0.6 x 10 = 6.
